title: Next steps in preparing for post-quantum cryptography
summary: NCSC whitepaper presenting post-quantum cryptography (PQC) as the best mitigation against the quantum threat to traditional public-key cryptography and setting out preparatory steps for organisations.

title: RFC 9794: a new standard for post-quantum terminology
summary: NCSC post introducing RFC 9794, a new standard for post-quantum cryptography terminology, in the context of NIST's publication of PQC standards.

title: Post-quantum cryptography (PQC) pilot under the Assured Cyber Security Consultancy scheme
summary: NCSC pilot scheme assuring consultancies that meet the scheme Standard for post-quantum cryptography; assured consultancies will support the NCSC's PQC work with organisations.

title: Timelines for migration to post-quantum cryptography
summary: NCSC guidance setting dated milestones for PQC migration: discovery by 2028, highest-priority migration by 2031, and complete migration of all systems, services and products by 2035.

title: Migrating to post-quantum cryptography (NCSC Annual Review 2025 chapter)
summary: Chapter of the NCSC Annual Review 2025 describing migration to post-quantum cryptography as an international effort to protect against a future large-scale quantum computer.

title: Post-quantum cryptography (NCSC Annual Review 2024 chapter)
summary: Chapter of the NCSC Annual Review 2024 identifying migration to post-quantum cryptography as the primary mitigation against the quantum threat to current cryptography.

title: Perspectives on the plan for PQC transition
summary: Independent research published on GOV.UK setting out regulator and industry perspectives on the current plan for the transition to post-quantum cryptography.

title: Quantum computing, sensing and communications (POSTnote PN 0742)
summary: Research briefing by the Parliamentary Office of Science and Technology noting that the UK National Cyber Security Centre has recommended organisations start moving to post-quantum cryptographic methods now.

title: Quantum Technology - parliamentary written question and answer (40037)
summary: Parliamentary written question and government answer on quantum technology, referencing guidance focused on migrating to post-quantum cryptography to mitigate the future quantum threat to encryption services.

title: Quantum Technology - parliamentary written question and answer (8496)
summary: Parliamentary written question and government answer noting that, following publication of post-quantum cryptography standards, the NCSC has published details of how it will help organisations plan their migration to PQC.

title: Google Quantum Computing Chip - House of Commons debate (Hansard)
summary: Hansard record of a House of Commons debate on quantum computing in which post-quantum cryptography is discussed as the means to prevent the so-called Y2Q ('year to quantum') end of privacy.

title: The Bank of England's approach to innovation in artificial intelligence, DLT and quantum computing
summary: Bank of England report on its approach to technological innovation, including developed risk scenarios for post-quantum threats such as encrypted data harvesting and quantum-enhanced trading.

title: A systemic risk perspective on operational resilience (speech)
summary: Bank of England keynote speech stating that, given firm dependencies on third-party vendors, the transition to post-quantum cryptography is broader than individual firms' risk appetites.

title: 2025 CBEST thematic
summary: Bank of England thematic findings from CBEST threat-led penetration testing, noting that the work supports organisations' progress toward post-quantum cryptography (PQC) readiness, with discovery as a key milestone.

title: Financial Stability Report - December 2025
summary: Bank of England Financial Stability Report discussing post-quantum cryptography (PQC) and urging firms to prepare and plan for PQC migration now, including mapping the software and hardware needed.

title: Guidance for Post-Quantum Cryptography (April 2025)
summary: Guidance from the UK Cross Market Operational Resilience Group (CMORG) for the financial sector on managing quantum risk, aligned with the UK National Cyber Security Centre's PQC guidance. CMORG is a financial-sector resilience group rather than a government body, so flagged for review.

title: Written evidence to a UK Parliament committee on quantum technologies (121374)
summary: Written evidence submitted to a UK parliamentary committee discussing post-quantum cryptography (PQC) algorithms as classical algorithms hoped to be secure against quantum computers. Submitter could not be identified from the candidate, so flagged for review.

title: Written evidence on quantum technologies (QUT0015)
summary: Written evidence submitted to a UK parliamentary inquiry on quantum technologies, defining post-quantum cryptography as classical cryptographic protocols believed to be resilient to quantum computing attacks. Submitter could not be identified from the candidate, so flagged for review.

title: John Taylor questionnaire, January 2024
summary: Appointment questionnaire published by the Bank of England noting that quantum computing could undermine standard methods for encrypting data and referencing post-quantum cryptography. PQC mention is incidental to the document's purpose, so flagged for review.

title: Enhancing the Privacy of a Digital Pound (MIT report)
summary: Report on privacy-enhancing technologies for a digital pound, hosted by the Bank of England, stating that assessment of PETs for CBDCs should consider compatibility with post-quantum security. Authored by MIT researchers rather than the Bank itself, so flagged for review.

title: Quantum key distribution research report
summary: Independent research commissioned by the Department for Science, Innovation and Technology (DSIT), published 10 June 2026, examining quantum key distribution (QKD) and its cyber security implications. It states that the UK NCSC advises post-quantum cryptography (PQC) as the preferred approach to the quantum threat and that QKD should be considered only as an additional layer alongside PQC, not a standalone solution.
